diagnosis: The player spent the first 6 seconds stationary at spawn (x=176, y=882) before moving, indicating a lack of immediate engagement. They then traversed a ladder-heavy vertical section with multiple ladders (marked 'L' in CSV) but showed significant backtracking: after reaching x≈1772 at t=18.8s, they fell to the bottom (x=265) by t=19.6s, then re-climbed the same path. Two combat deaths occurred at x=1817/y=532 and x=2495/y=443 — both in narrow corridors with enemies on elevated platforms (digits 1-9 in CSV), consistent with lesson_0003. The player stalled for 8+ seconds at x=2294 (t=39–42s) near a dense enemy cluster, then fell again, and finally disengaged at spawn (x=73) for the last 6 seconds. Level not completed in 75.5s with 2 deaths and 5 hits taken.

lessons.lesson: Multiple visually similar ladders in a vertical corridor without signposting cause players to repeatedly ascend the wrong shaft, fall, and re-climb, wasting time and increasing perceived difficulty.
conditions: vertical corridor with 3+ ladders in close horizontal proximity
no visual differentiation (color, markers, lighting) between ladders
critical path requires specific ladder sequence
lessons.outcome: player backtracks vertically, stalls at dead ends, re-traverses same segments, and disengages

lessons.lesson: Enemy clusters placed at ladder tops or corridor chokepoints where the player must commit to a jump or climb create unavoidable damage zones that feel unfair, not challenging.
conditions: enemies positioned at ladder exits or narrow corridor entries
player has no invincibility frames or dodge tools during climb/jump commitment
no safe observation point before engagement
lessons.outcome: repeated combat deaths at same locations, high hits taken, player stalls or quits
